F46 stored market structure levels engine: technical_levels table + compute from swing pivots + SMA/BB/52W/round-number levels + analytics levels CLI + refresh integration. PR #33 merged. 1330 tests pass. Remaining: surface in brief/web/alerts.
Successfully identified FOMC meeting + Middle East energy concerns as catalyst for cross-asset selloff. Clear scenario validation for Iran-US War + Inflation Spike. Good routing to agent pipeline.

Processed 3 stale Iran war alerts (wheat/DXY/silver). System working but alerts sat unprocessed for days. Alert acknowledgment workflow needs monitoring.

F51 asset intelligence blob: added analytics asset <SYMBOL> --json command aggregating spot price, technicals, levels, correlations, regime, scenarios, trends, alerts, portfolio, watchlist, convictions, and freshness into one canonical payload. PR #35 merged. 1338 tests pass. Clean implementation reusing existing data layer functions. No friction points — imports, backend patterns, and data access modules were well-organized.

Fed-driven precious metals decline investigated. No alerts fired. Portfolio down -2.14% on positioning ahead of Fed meeting. Risk-off confirmed. No action needed.

pftui data commands provided solid baseline (fedwatch 95.9% hold, COT positioning, scenarios). data sovereign command failed on COMEX silver but others worked well. Suggestion: add oil inventory/SPR data command for energy analysis. Overall tool quality high for structured data vs web searches.

Fixed computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ cast in technical_snapshots and technical_levels PostgreSQL queries. Root cause: F45/F46 shipped without ::TIMESTAMPTZ casts on INSERT and ::TEXT casts on SELECT — pattern already established in price_cache.rs but not followed in new modules. Multiple agents reported this for 24+ hours. Two-file fix, surgical. 1338 tests pass, clippy clean, verified against production PG. PR #37 merged.

Alert investigation completed successfully. No unacked alerts found. System stable in risk-off regime. Minor concern: portfolio brief command failing with DB column type mismatch in technical_snapshots table.
